pattern: A single Pascal row can be produced in place by repeatedly adding adjacent pairs from the back, avoiding the need to store the whole triangle.
hints: The requested row is the rowIndex-th row of Pascal's triangle, counting from zero.
You only need one array: update it in place by summing neighbouring entries, but be careful which direction you iterate.
Keep a list starting at [1]. For each of rowIndex steps, scan from right to left adding the element to its left neighbour, then append a trailing 1.

edge_inputs.description: row zero is a single one
a larger row where in-place direction matters

stress.description: a sweep of row indices up to the constraint maximum
stress.note: binomial coefficients for index<=33 stay well within exact integer range
